rationale: Fidelity/deferral re-verification at 8b945359. The two round-1 changes I own the lens for both hold up. (B) The findRoot carve-out was cleanly relocated Out-of-Scope→In-Scope: R3 now removes all three carve-outs, the Scope In-Scope bullet says 'all three', and the sole Out-of-Scope removal is exactly the old findRoot deferral bullet — nothing silently dropped, no report scope invented. The reinstatement is guarded by (A) new R11, a hard 107-prerequisite that documents the merge-resolution order for internal/validate/plan.go and makes the findRoot removal inert post-rebase; R12 codifies bead decomposition. These are sequencing/planning requirements, not new feature scope. I re-confirmed the base state on the tree: .golangci.yml still carries all four carve-outs (brownfield:77, isNeighbor:84, findRoot:88, validateReviewMode:92), findRoot() is still present (next/beads.go:53), and IsDomainCoveredCtx is still live (plan.go:647) — all confirming 107 is unmerged in 108's base, so R3's gating and R11's ordering are correct. Out-of-Scope completeness otherwise unchanged: DRY #2/#4/#6-18, Perf #2/#3/#6/#7, internal/frontmatter, other unowned paths, and non-documented behavioral changes all remain explicitly excluded. My round-1 cross-spec plan.go-overlap note is now resolved by R11. APPROVE stands.
findings: RE-VERIFIED (round-1 asks): the findRoot deferral moved Out-of-Scope→In-Scope with no fidelity loss. R3 now targets all three stale carve-outs (brownfield, isNeighbor, findRoot); In-Scope bullet updated to 'all three'; the old Out-of-Scope findRoot bullet is the ONLY excision from Out of Scope, and it is fully accounted for by the In-Scope move. AC grep updated consistently: asserts all three absent AND validateReviewMode retained (the retained-carve-out assertion correctly switched from 'findRoot' in round 1 to 'validateReviewMode').

VERIFIED base-state (fidelity anchor for R3/R11): .golangci.yml carve-outs at brownfield/plan:77, isNeighbor:84, findRoot:88, validateReviewMode:92; findRoot() present at internal/next/beads.go:53; IsDomainCoveredCtx present at internal/validate/plan.go:647; SpecStatusFromBytes at frontmatter.go:46. All confirm 107 is unmerged in 108's base — so R3's 'gated on R11 rebase' and R11's ordering claim are factually correct, not aspirational.

OUT-OF-SCOPE COMPLETENESS holds: every item I inventoried in round 1 remains excluded — DRY #2/#4/#6-18, Perf #2/#3/#6/#7, internal/frontmatter (unowned, read-only), all other unowned paths (otel/frontmatter), and behavioral changes beyond the two documented (R5 fence-strictness, R6 approval source-of-truth). No new silent omissions introduced by the round-2 edits.

CROSS-SPEC NOTE RESOLVED: my round-1 note about 107↔108 both touching internal/validate/plan.go is now explicitly handled by R11 — 107's IsDomainCoveredCtx deletion lands first, 108's parsePlanFrontmatter (R5) applies on top, and 107's domain-doc appends precede 108's so gate-forwardness appends stack. R12 further requires 108's own beads' doc appends be unique across 108 while allowed to share files with 107's.

ADVISORY (carried from round 1, non-blocking): Out of Scope labels DRY #2 'setup override-flag validation dedup' — DRY #2 is cmd complete.go/impl.go override flags, not setup. The DRY #2 reference is explicit and correct; only the 'setup' word is a mislabel. Cosmetic; no gate impact.

ADVISORY (carried from round 1, non-blocking): the spec's rebuttal of the review's 'used by recording/otel' trace characterization is empirically correct but is not verbatim in the tracked review-source-report.md (which only calls MarshalJSON an 'aliased pass-through'). Harmless — the ownership decision is independently justified by the import graph. No change required.

ADVISORY (round-2, non-blocking): R11's precondition ('107 merged to main AND spec/108 rebased onto post-107 main before plan approve') is a lifecycle gate the orchestrator must enforce manually — mindspec has no automated base-ancestry check at plan-approve. The spec documents the operator obligation clearly (implementing bead MUST verify beads.go contains no findRoot before deleting that carve-out), which is the right mitigation for a spec-doc; flagging only so the plan/impl gates keep it visible.
